product_context: ElevenLabs is the leading AI voice technology company. Core products: Text-to-Speech API (ultra-realistic voices in 32 languages, 3000+ voices, latency as low as 75ms), Voice Cloning (professional and instant cloning from 1 min of audio), Voice Library (community marketplace), AI Dubbing (automatic video/audio translation preserving original speaker voice, 29 languages), Conversational AI (voice agents for support, sales, onboarding), and Reader App (iOS/Android, reads any content aloud). Key integrations: REST API, Python/JS SDKs, WebSocket streaming, partnerships with enterprise platforms. Used for content creation, customer support automation, accessibility, gaming, media localization, and IVR systems.

text_to_speech_api.name: Text to Speech API
text_to_speech_api.description: High-volume programmatic text-to-speech with ultra-realistic voices. 32 languages, 3000+ voices, latency as low as 75ms. Supports streaming and batch processing via REST API and WebSocket.
best_for: Accessibility features for apps and websites

voice_cloning.name: Voice Cloning
voice_cloning.description: Clone any voice from as little as 1 minute of audio. Two tiers: Instant Clone (quick, good quality) and Professional Clone (studio-grade, requires 30+ minutes of clean audio). Create consistent brand voices or preserve creator voices across content.
best_for: Media companies needing consistent narrator voices
Brand voice for marketing and communications
Dubbing own content while preserving voice identity

dubbing_api.name: Dubbing API
dubbing_api.description: Automatically translate and re-voice video/audio content into 29 languages while preserving the original speaker's voice, tone, and emotion. End-to-end pipeline: transcription, translation, voice synthesis, and lip-sync.
best_for: YouTube creators expanding to global audiences
Corporate training videos for global teams

conversational_ai.name: Conversational AI
conversational_ai.description: Build human-like voice agents for phone and web. Ultra-low latency voice interaction for customer support, sales, onboarding, and more. Integrates with existing telephony (SIP/PSTN), CRMs, and knowledge bases.

reader_app.name: Reader App
reader_app.description: iOS and Android app that reads any content aloud with premium AI voices. Users can listen to articles, PDFs, newsletters, and web content. White-label and API options available for publishers.

murf.name: Murf.ai
murf.focus: Studio-based TTS for marketing and e-learning
murf.weakness: Limited API capabilities, fewer languages, no real-time streaming

play_ht.name: Play.ht
play_ht.weakness: Lower voice quality, limited enterprise features, no dubbing

resemble_ai.name: Resemble.ai
resemble_ai.weakness: Smaller voice library, less language coverage, niche market

wellsaid.name: WellSaid Labs
wellsaid.focus: Enterprise TTS for training and corporate video
wellsaid.weakness: Limited to English, no dubbing, no conversational AI

lovo_ai.name: Lovo.ai
lovo_ai.weakness: Consumer-focused, limited API, fewer enterprise integrations

speechify.name: Speechify
speechify.weakness: Consumer product, limited API/enterprise offering, no voice cloning

deepgram.name: Deepgram
deepgram.weakness: Primarily STT company, TTS is newer and less mature, no dubbing or cloning

azure_tts.name: Azure Cognitive Services TTS
azure_tts.weakness: Less natural voice quality, complex pricing, requires Azure commitment, no dubbing

differentiators: Best-in-class voice quality — consistently rated most natural-sounding AI voices
Ultra-low latency (75ms) enabling real-time conversational use cases
Broadest language coverage (32 languages) with native-quality output
Voice cloning fidelity — Professional Clone captures emotion, cadence, and accent
End-to-end dubbing pipeline — no other vendor offers transcription + translation + voice preservation
Simple, developer-friendly API with generous free tier for evaluation
Scales from prototype to millions of characters per month without architecture changes
SOC 2 Type II compliant with enterprise security features

cost.objection: ElevenLabs is more expensive than alternatives
cost.response: Our per-character pricing is competitive at scale, and the quality difference eliminates the need for manual audio editing or re-recording. Customers typically see 60-80% reduction in total audio production cost. We offer volume discounts and custom enterprise pricing.

integration_complexity.objection: Integration seems complex / we don't have engineering bandwidth
integration_complexity.response: Our API is designed for simplicity — most integrations take 2-3 days, not weeks. We provide SDKs for Python, JavaScript, and other languages, plus a dedicated FDE to support your integration. Many customers go from first API call to production in under a week.

voice_ownership.objection: Who owns the cloned voice? What about IP and rights?
voice_ownership.response: You retain full ownership of any voice you clone. We require consent verification for voice cloning to protect all parties. Our terms explicitly state that generated audio and cloned voice models belong to the customer. We're the industry leader in ethical voice AI practices.

data_privacy.objection: Where is our data stored? GDPR/compliance concerns
data_privacy.response: We're SOC 2 Type II compliant. Data can be processed in US or EU regions. We offer data processing agreements (DPAs) and can accommodate specific data residency requirements for enterprise customers. No training on customer data without explicit opt-in.

quality_concerns.objection: AI voices still sound robotic / not good enough for our brand
quality_concerns.response: We encourage a blind listening test — our voices are regularly indistinguishable from human recordings in A/B tests. We can run a proof-of-concept with your actual content so you can evaluate quality firsthand before any commitment.

content_platform.example: A major news publisher uses ElevenLabs to auto-generate audio for 500+ articles daily, increasing engagement by 40% and time-on-site by 25%.

gaming.example: An indie game studio cloned 12 unique character voices, reducing voiceover production time from 6 months to 3 weeks and cutting costs by 90%.

education.example: A global e-learning platform generates course narration in 15 languages from a single English script, launching localized courses 10x faster.

media_localization.example: A YouTube creator with 2M subscribers uses Dubbing API to auto-translate videos into 8 languages, growing international viewership by 300%.

customer_support.example: A telecom company replaced their legacy IVR with ElevenLabs Conversational AI, deflecting 45% of Tier 1 calls and improving CSAT from 6.1 to 8.3.

publishing.example: A newsletter with 200K subscribers added a 'Listen' button powered by ElevenLabs, seeing 35% of readers switch to audio format during commute hours.

eleven_multilingual_v2.name: Eleven Multilingual v2
eleven_multilingual_v2.description: Highest quality multilingual TTS model. 29 languages with native-quality output.

eleven_turbo_v2_5.name: Eleven Turbo v2.5
eleven_turbo_v2_5.description: Lowest latency model (~75ms). Optimized for real-time conversational use cases.

eleven_english_v2.name: Eleven English v2
eleven_english_v2.description: Highest quality English-only model. Best for English-only deployments where quality is paramount.

scribe_v2.name: Scribe v2
scribe_v2.description: Batch transcription model for subtitling, captioning, and offline speech-to-text.

scribe_v2_realtime.name: Scribe v2 Realtime
scribe_v2_realtime.description: Ultra-low latency live STT with VAD commit. Ideal for voice agent pipelines needing live transcription.

rest_api_batch.name: REST API (batch TTS)
rest_api_batch.description: Simplest integration. Async content generation via HTTP POST. Best for non-real-time use cases.

streaming_api.name: Streaming API
streaming_api.description: Low latency real-time TTS via chunked HTTP streaming. Good for interactive but not conversational use cases.

websocket_scribe_realtime.name: WebSocket (Scribe v2 Realtime)
websocket_scribe_realtime.description: Live transcription via WebSocket. Use in Advanced Cascaded architecture for voice agent STT.

elevenagents_platform.name: ElevenAgents Platform
elevenagents_platform.description: No-code/low-code agent builder with native Twilio/phone integration. Fastest path to voice agent deployment.

react_sdk.name: React SDK (@elevenlabs/react)
react_sdk.description: Client-side streaming with single-use token auth. Best for web apps needing in-browser voice.

twilio.name: Twilio
twilio.description: Native SIP/PSTN integration for voice agents. Direct replacement for Twilio + Polly/Google TTS stacks.

chrome_ai_translator.name: Chrome AI Translator API
chrome_ai_translator.description: Client-side translation integration for web-based dubbing and localization workflows.

webhooks.name: Standard Webhooks
webhooks.description: Webhook support for agent handoffs, status callbacks, and event-driven audio generation.

Discovery: What voice/audio workflows does your team manage today?
How many languages do you need to support?
What's your current TTS or voice solution?
What's the volume of audio content you produce monthly?
Who owns the voice/audio pipeline internally?
Technical Evaluation: What's your current API infrastructure look like?
Do you need real-time streaming or batch processing?
What latency requirements do you have for voice generation?
How do you handle content moderation for generated audio?
What's your deployment environment (cloud, on-prem, hybrid)?
Pilot: Which use case should we prioritize for the pilot?
What does success look like in the first 30 days?
Who on your team will be the technical lead for integration?
What volume of API calls do you expect during the pilot?
How will you measure quality of the generated voice output?
Negotiation: What's your budget range for voice AI infrastructure?
Do you need an enterprise SLA or dedicated support?
Are there compliance or data residency requirements?
Would a multi-year commitment work for better pricing?
Deployment: What's your rollout plan across teams/products?
Do you need custom voice models or fine-tuning?
How will you monitor usage and quality in production?
What training does your team need on the API?
Who should be the ongoing technical point of contact?
